g.data-narration: Welcome to Web Security Considerations. We'll explore threats, vulnerabilities, and the layered defenses that protect modern web applications.
Web security matters because every day, millions of attacks target web applications. Understanding these threats motivates why we need protocols like TLS.
The CIA triad forms the foundation of web security: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. These three principles guide all security decisions.

g.text: Data accessible only to authorized users

g.data-narration: Web threats are classified into passive attacks like eavesdropping, and active attacks like modification and denial of service. Each requires different defenses.

g.text: Attacker only observes, does not modify

g.data-narration: Network-level threats include eavesdropping where attackers intercept communications, MITM where they intercept and alter messages, and DoS attacks that overwhelm services.
Application-level threats exploit vulnerabilities in web code: XSS injects malicious scripts, SQL injection manipulates databases, and CSRF tricks users into unwanted actions.
g.g: <text x="380" y="538" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" white">SQL Injection Database manipulation Extracts sensitive data

g.text: ➤ Session Hijacking: Steals session tokens to impersonate users

g.data-narration: Web applications must meet security requirements: authentication verifies identities, access control limits permissions, and non-repudiation ensures actions cannot be denied.
SSL/TLS provides the primary solution for securing web communications. It encrypts data in transit, authenticates servers, and ensures message integrity through cryptographic protocols.
Defense in depth uses layered security: network firewalls and IDS at the edge, SSL/TLS for transport encryption, and application-level input validation and authentication.
Common web vulnerabilities include injection flaws, broken authentication, sensitive data exposure, XML external entities, and security misconfigurations that attackers exploit.
g.text: Other: A5-Broken Access Control | A6-Security Misconfig | A7-XSS | A8-Insecure Deserialization | A9-Using Components | A10-Insufficient Logging

g.data-narration: In summary, web security requires understanding threats at all layers, implementing the CIA triad, using SSL/TLS for transport security, and applying defense in depth strategies.
g.text: Active: MITM, DoS, XSS, SQLi, CSRF
CIA Triad + SSL/TLS + Defense in Depth

g.data-narration: For exams, remember the difference between passive and active attacks, know how TLS provides confidentiality and integrity, and understand the layered security model.

g.text: → Study the layered security approach (Network → Transport → Application) ←
